item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Y'know, it'd be a shame if they never did manage to upgrade to the Xbox WAN shot

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
360 controlers work with PCs…. they should be hooked up to a PC and allowed to play the new games to see what happens.

Across LAN only!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is wonderful. I really hope that Foundation will let them play new CoD games.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Do you think this branch of the CotBG could help me get a direct cranial connection with my Steam account?

+1

item.title: STAFF POST - OPEN Crosslink Proposal
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

Show Reasoning
hide reasoning

The crosslink below is being proposed based on the Author's headcanon.

Editing this:

God's in us.

To this:

God's in us.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't play COD, but now every time I die because I'm outplayed, I can just say "Must be something like SCP-2360."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What's AWCY about it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This feels like a cross between Are we cool yet? and the church of the broken God(Which is already there).

Jeez, Maybe they could give them other than COD.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can imagine 2360-A, B & C playing against 2662 (Cluthu f'UCK OFF) in an Foundation arranged event. And I can see Bright being the Bookie for the event.
Screw it… I'm writing a Tale Hub for a Video Game Tournament which the Foundation had set up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While the former is perfectly valid criticism (although I'm curious as to what makes it that painful to read for you), the latter is objectively wrong. There are plenty of whimsical articles, and this isn't even one of them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is painful to read and it's a stain on the Foundation's serious tone. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
jesus christ, this is amazing, +1.
Here's hoping the Foundation scores a 1540-noscope-nolegs-midair-headshot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: CotBG Call of Duty players. Brilliant. +1 all the way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As mentioned in chat, this article has officially outdone those cows in terms of "stupidest thing to hit the mainlist". And you know what? It's glorious, in more than one way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You don't say.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: X-box 360, actually.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
OH MY GOD
I JUST GOT SOMETHING
THIS SCP IS 2360.
AS IN 360 NOSCOPING.
YOU MOTHER FUCKER

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think a little more than vegetable oil has been "brominated" here. This is a bit silly, but I can see a group of CoD-pieces getting mixed up with the CotBG and coming to this conclusion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Awesome article! I love the idea and the execution is wonderful.

One thing though, the last sentence of the Special Containment Procedures reads:

"If SCP-2360 begins to exhibit signs of an impending Nibbanic event, sever network connection immediately and execute the Upasana Procedure."

It seems like there should be more words in the italicized section.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And yet no Doritos. Wonder how they feel about the PC master race.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Poor Wallace. Red ring of deathed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just a little too silly for me, I'm afraid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tras-humanist COD Bros… I love it! +1

*confetti and kazoos*

I love this take on the CotBG.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually disagree; there's no real reason to explain it in the article, because it's clear from context what it means for the Foundation at least.

(also, this is great)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You had me when they subsisted on high fructose corn syrup and caffeine… fucking brilliance haha.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted, and this seems like it was inspired by someone saying "come play me at Call of Duty."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Planned obsolescence: the bane of cyborgs everywhere.

item.title: And now it is recorded, forever more.
item.content:encoded:
Stupid, they said... Useless, they said...
Look at the Random Idea Generator now!

<Silberescher> …shit
<Spidersnake> I thought it was just for fucking around.
<weizhong> See ya folks
<Silberescher> I just realized
<Zolgamax> Hokay, here goes:
<Spidersnake> goodbye
<Silberescher> I had totally forgotten
<Bryx> goodbye.
<Silberescher> !scp-2360 was based on the random generator
<+Nala> Silberescher: Cog of Duty (rating: +70, 5 revisions) - http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-2360

  • weizhong (effarig.a|mi.oom#effarig.a|mi.oom) Quit (Quit: READ THIS: http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-1974)

<Silberescher> it was something like "Anomalous humanoids with their eyes replaced with clockwork that ascend unless you own. The article should explore isolation."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love it, gotta wonder whether you could add Dorito dust to the liquid feed for increased performance. Anyway, I think interpolation might not be used correctly here. Technically interpolation is a method of upscaling. It sounds cool, though. Maybe HDMI mux/overlay? Still sounds technical, would mean their eye implants can take an HDMI signal and display it to the optic nerve either overlaid, replaced, or windowed with their existing input (physical sight, whatever "heads-up display" data their implants are generating).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I second what Shen said. I had to look it up to figure out what it meant and I don't think I should have had to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still feel it should be explained in the article. Unless there's a direct link, the articles should be able to stand on their own and make sense. 'course, that's just my oppinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I forgot to say how much I loved this when I first upvoted it. I'm saying it now because I also have a question: Which SCP causes melting doors to smell like Maple syrup and ass?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great. I love the idea of a "forward-thinking" sect of the CotBG a lot more than I thought I would.

I've seen some people claim that this should be an SCP-J article, but I disagree. It's pretty dang unsettling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A bunch of guys to try to achieve transcendence by playing Call of Duty and live off of Mountain Dew. I dig it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did you just-

i hate you.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now let's see them start a console war…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's related to this SCP, which has the mainlist title "Post-Nibbanic". "Nibbana" is an alternate spelling of "Nirvana"; a "Nibbanic event" is basically whatever these guys mean by "reaching heaven". The Foundation may or may not know what that would actually entail, but they have plans to stop it in either case. The "Upasana" in "Upasana Procedure" is a Sanskrit term used in Buddhism to refer to attachment to mortal things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ok, I must've missed it. Can someone explain for me what the Nibbanic even -is-?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like that we have a more moderate strain of the CotBG that could actually have reasonable, non-cultist people signing up.

Now I want to do something about CotBG presence in Silicon Valley. I can really see this catching on there…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this aspect of the CotBG. Crazy, no doubt, but also not violent and such towards the non-converted. Rather nice to see with all the "Kill the Heretics" CotBG stuff we have.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was a little iffy on this until the "religious persecution" part, and then the interviews just nailed it. I love the use of CotBG here, as well as the riffing on online gamers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Never thought I'd see anything like this from the CotBG. Also never thought I'd want anything like this from the CotBG. Now both those points are moot. Take your +1 you magnificent person.

item.title: Oh my god you posted the COG gamers.
item.content:encoded: Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really good.

item.title: Gotta get them xXx[MLG]SN1P3R_420xXx swagshots
item.content:encoded:
a specially-formulated liquid feed, taken orally, which contains high-fructose corn syrup, caffeine, orange juice, sodium benzoate, and brominated vegetable oil, among other ingredients.

All about that Double XP Mountain Dew man

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought I was going to hate this.

And honestly, I still do. But I can't downvote it. I can't.

item.title: Director's Notes
item.content:encoded:
Show Rambling
Hide Rambling

This is something I came up with in chat, like most of my SCP ideas. I don't know what exactly was going on that sparked it, but later on in the writing process, I realized that I was being influenced largely by Super Hexagon. For the unfamiliar, it's like a rhythm game in structure - you move according to shapes that come at you in time with the music - but the actual gameplay is closer to Super Meat Boy; you die and restart repeatedly until pattern recognition takes over and you start reacting to events in the game before they register with you. Zen-like focus.

I keep coming up with extra bits of story for these guys that I'm not going to clutter up the actual article with, so I'm going to post them here:

  • Innovationism is a relatively small and newly-formed sect of the COTBG, created as a reaction to the changing relationship between humanity and machines. While it's not much now, it grows in relevance and popularity as topics like transhumanism reach the forefront of societal discussion, and it becomes the religion followed by the U.S. President in 2040, W. W. Solenoid, as mentioned in SCP-2005.
  • This particular church father claims to be a former missionary who emigrated from China due to religious persecution. In fact, he escaped due to a situation regarding his work with the Chaos Insurgency, whose progressive attitude about human augmentation has led to cooperation with members of the Church, as well as the Chinese government, who has dabbled with this sort of thing before.
  • In case you didn't notice, the "liquid feed" 2360 uses isn't arbitrary in composition. Look it up.
  • The electronic components of 2360 run on a proprietary COTBG operating system named Apotheo-Sys.

EDIT: Thanks to PeppersGhost for coming up with the mainlist name.

item.title: I said I was only going to make the one post but this article is just too fucking brilliant
item.content:encoded: The best part is that they run on Mountain Dew. That's all I'm gonna say.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed, apologies for the delay.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like the re-imagining of the CotBG as a more moderate, technologically oriented movement. I think it adds quite a bit.

But these should really be Euclid. :L

+1 anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Brilliant.
